steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Start with the given equation: $p = \frac{k}{5j+8}$. The goal is to isolate $5j+8$ on one side.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $(5j+8)$ to remove it from the denominator: $p(5j+8) = k$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Divide both sides by $p$ to isolate $5j+8$: $5j+8 = \frac{k}{p}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate each answer choice:
  • Choice A — $5j+8=\frac{k}{p}$: matches the derived result exactly. That confirms A is correct.
  • Choice B — $5j+8=kp$: this would mean multiplying both sides by $p$ without dividing, giving $p^2(5j+8) = kp$, which is wrong. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — $5j+8=k-p$: subtraction never appears in the algebraic manipulation. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $5j+8=\frac{p}{k}$: inverts the fraction incorrectly; numerator and denominator are swapped. Eliminated.
